$schema_note: The SYNTAX side of the per-token state machine. The morphology side fixes WHAT the token is (a surface + POS + reading); this file fixes the syntactic ROLE that reading takes in the sentence, given a context trigger. A transition fires ONLY when the context_trigger is observed; otherwise the token stays in its unresolved state (pending beats a wrong role). Each transition carries requires_two_vote: when true, the role may not be authored/applied until two independent checks agree on BOTH conclusion and reasoning (see ../rules/two-vote-required-rules.json two_vote_protocol). forbidden_transitions capture homograph collisions that share letters under norm() but are different lexemes — read names with norm_strict/bare/QAC POS, never the recall-only norm(). A single forbidden gloss is given per collision so the wrong role can never be authored even when the diacritic is present.
generator: authored by hand; aligned to ../examples/function-word-decisions.jsonl and ../examples/ayah-context-decisions.jsonl; cross-checks ../rules/particle-context-rules.json (haraka split), ../rules/context-sense-rules.json (sense), ../rules/referent-guard-rules.json (referent), ../rules/two-vote-required-rules.json (gate)
principle: A reading does not name its own syntactic role; the governing context does. Fire a transition only on its trigger, attach the gate the role demands, and quarantine homograph siblings via forbidden_transitions. PENDING beats a wrong role.

state_legend.from_observation: the morphologically-fixed reading the token enters with (surface + POS + diacritic-resolved identity), the OUTPUT of particle-context-rules.json — not a raw norm() surface
state_legend.context_trigger: the syntactic signal in the surrounding tokens that licenses the transition (following POS/case, governing partner, construct head, preceding particle)
state_legend.to_nahw_role: the iʿrāb role the token takes once the trigger is observed
state_legend.requires_two_vote: true → role carries a two_vote_required gate (it appears in ../rules/two-vote-required-rules.json two_vote_triggers); author/apply only on two-agreeing-checks
state_legend.else: if the trigger is absent the token holds at pending with the stated pending_reason

transitions.from_observation: أَنْ (light, no shadda; hamza-above seat confirmed by norm_strict)
transitions.context_trigger: an imperfect verb follows in naṣb (subjunctive), e.g. أَنْ تَصُومُوا
transitions.to_nahw_role: ḥarf maṣdariyyah wa-naṣb — subjunctival 'that/to' forming a maṣdar muʾawwal; nuṣiba al-fiʿl baʿdahā
transitions.reason: light أَنْ governs the following fiʿl muḍāriʿ into naṣb; the role is the subjunctive operator, not the emphatic أَنَّ

else.note: if the following verb's mood is unmarked, do not assert naṣb

transitions.from_observation: أَنَّ (heavy, shadda on nūn; hamza-above seat)
transitions.context_trigger: a noun/pronoun follows that it puts in naṣb as its ism, then a khabar, e.g. عَلِمَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ
transitions.to_nahw_role: min akhawāt inna — ḥarf tawkīd wa-naṣb; tansibu al-ism wa-tarfaʿu al-khabar, the clause is a maṣdar muʾawwal object
transitions.reason: heavy أَنَّ heads a noun-clause and assigns naṣb to its ism; distinct from light أَنْ which takes a verb

else.note: needs the ism in naṣb to confirm the akhawāt-inna role

transitions.from_observation: إِنَّ (heavy, shadda on nūn; hamza-BELOW seat)
transitions.context_trigger: sentence-initial / clause-initial before its ism in naṣb, e.g. إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَعَ ٱلصَّابِرِينَ
transitions.to_nahw_role: ḥarf tawkīd wa-naṣb (umm al-bāb) — 'indeed/verily'; tansibu al-ism wa-tarfaʿu al-khabar
transitions.reason: kasra-seat emphatic إِنَّ asserts the clause; the ism takes naṣb, the khabar rafʿ

else.note: if norm() dropped the hamza seat, re-read with norm_strict before fixing إِنَّ vs أَنَّ

transitions.context_trigger: a jussive verb (or two jazm-verb clauses: sharṭ + jawāb) follows, e.g. إِنْ تَنْصُرُوا ٱللَّهَ يَنْصُرْكُمْ
transitions.to_nahw_role: adāt sharṭ jāzima — conditional 'if'; tajzimu fiʿlayn (fiʿl al-sharṭ wa-jawābih)
transitions.reason: light conditional إِنْ governs both verbs into jazm; it is neither the emphatic إِنَّ nor the negating إِنْ

else.note: without a jussive jawāb the conditional vs negating reading of light إِنْ is open

transitions.from_observation: مَن (pronoun; fatḥa/bare on mīm, no shadda on nūn — per particle-context-rules man_vs_min)
transitions.context_trigger: heads a relative/conditional clause with a following ṣilah verb, e.g. مَن يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ
transitions.to_nahw_role: ism mawṣūl / sharṭ — relative-or-conditional pronoun 'whoever'; mabnī, holds a rafʿ/naṣb maḥall by its clause function
transitions.reason: the pronoun مَن takes its role from the clause it heads; the haraka split (not min) is already settled upstream

else.note: if the mīm harakah is absent, مَن (pronoun) vs مِن (preposition) is unsettled — do not assign a role

transitions.from_observation: مِنْ (preposition; kasra on mīm — incl. وَمِنَ)
transitions.context_trigger: a noun in jarr follows, e.g. مِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ
transitions.to_nahw_role: ḥarf jarr — forms a jar-majrūr; the following ism is majrūr
transitions.reason: kasra-mīm preposition governs the following noun into jarr; role is the prepositional phrase

else.note: if the governed noun's case is unmarked, hold before asserting jarr

transitions.from_observation: لَا followed by an indefinite noun in naṣb without tanwīn
transitions.context_trigger: an indefinite mabnī noun in naṣb immediately follows, e.g. لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ / لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ
transitions.to_nahw_role: lā al-nāfiyah lil-jins — absolute genus-negation 'there is no … at all'; tabnī al-ism al-mufrad ʿalā al-fatḥ
transitions.reason: لا + indefinite naṣb-noun negates the whole genus; this is a distinct operator from verbal negation, see ../rules/context-sense-rules.json la_nafiya_lil_jins and ../rules/negation-rules.json

else.note: if a verb (not an indefinite noun) follows لا, this is verbal negation, not lil-jins

transitions.context_trigger: a mustathnā follows after a mustathnā-minhu, e.g. فَشَرِبُوا مِنْهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّنْهُمْ
transitions.to_nahw_role: adāt istithnāʾ — the mustathnā is manṣūb in a complete affirmative sentence (kalām tāmm mūjab); after negation/incomplete it may follow the badal/maḥall rule
transitions.reason: istithnāʾ case assignment depends on tāmm/nāqiṣ and mūjab/manfī; never default the mustathnā to naṣb without checking the sentence type

else.note: negative/incomplete exception flips the case rule — resolve sentence completeness first

transitions.from_observation: a definite/indefinite noun immediately followed by a second noun
transitions.context_trigger: the second noun is in jarr with no intervening particle and the first carries no tanwīn/nūn, e.g. كِتَابُ ٱللَّهِ
transitions.to_nahw_role: muḍāf + muḍāf ilayh — possessive construct; the muḍāf drops tanwīn/nūn, the muḍāf ilayh is majrūr
transitions.reason: noun-noun adjacency is iḍāfa only when the second is majrūr and the first is stripped; otherwise it may be naʿt or two separate roles

else.note: if the first noun keeps tanwīn or the second is not majrūr, it is not iḍāfa — could be mawṣūf+ṣifah

transitions.from_observation: لَمَّا (shadda on mīm — per particle-context-rules lima_vs_lamma)
transitions.context_trigger: a past verb follows it and a second past clause answers it, e.g. فَلَمَّا جَاءَهُمُ ٱلْحَقُّ
transitions.to_nahw_role: ẓarf / ḥarf wujūb li-wujūb — temporal 'when' binding two past clauses (sharṭ ghayr jāzim)
transitions.reason: shadda-mīm لَمَّا heads a temporal subordination over two māḍī clauses; distinct from لِمَا (lām + relative mā)

else.note: no shadda on the mīm → لِمَا (for/to that which), not the temporal لَمَّا

forbidden_transitions.forbidden_to: ism mawṣūl / sharṭ (the relative-pronoun role of مَن)
forbidden_transitions.why_forbidden: the kasra-mīm preposition can never take a relative-pronoun role; its only role is ḥarf jarr. Reading it as مَن swaps a particle for a pronoun on a bare haraka difference.
forbidden_transitions.correct_instead: ḥarf jarr → jar-majrūr (gloss: from / among / of)
forbidden_transitions.guard: read the mīm harakah via norm_strict; وَمِنَ is NOT وَمَن

forbidden_transitions.forbidden_from: لَمْ (negative particle + jazm, sukūn on mīm)
forbidden_transitions.why_forbidden: لَمْ negates a past verb via jussive and is never an interrogative; assigning it the question role drops the jazm and invents a question that is not there.
forbidden_transitions.correct_instead: ḥarf nafy wa-jazm → jussive negation (gloss: did not / has not)
forbidden_transitions.guard: fatḥa-on-lām + jussive verb marks لَمْ; kasra-on-lām marks لِمَ

forbidden_transitions.why_forbidden: shadda-mīm لَمَّا is a temporal subordinator over two clauses; reading it as لِ + مَا turns a 'when'-clause into a prepositional phrase and loses the two-clause binding.
forbidden_transitions.correct_instead: ẓarf/ḥarf wujūb li-wujūb → temporal 'when / after / not yet'
forbidden_transitions.guard: the shadda on the mīm is decisive; absent shadda → لِمَا, present → لَمَّا

forbidden_transitions.collision: أَنَّ vs إِنَّ (norm surface ان — hamza seat collapsed)
forbidden_transitions.forbidden_from: أَنَّ (hamza-above, opens a maṣdar-muʾawwal object-clause mid-sentence)
forbidden_transitions.why_forbidden: أَنَّ heads a subordinate noun-clause governed by a preceding verb (عَلِمَ أَنَّ…); إِنَّ stands at the head of an independent assertion. Swapping the seat re-roots the clause from a complement to a standalone assertion.
forbidden_transitions.correct_instead: min akhawāt inna heading a maṣdar muʾawwal (gloss: that)
forbidden_transitions.guard: hamza-above (أ) = أَنَّ subordinate; hamza-below (إ) = إِنَّ initial — preserved by norm_strict, dropped by norm()

forbidden_transitions.collision: إِنْ (light conditional) vs أَنْ (light subjunctive) (norm surface ان)
forbidden_transitions.why_forbidden: light إِنْ governs TWO verbs into jazm as a conditional; light أَنْ governs ONE verb into naṣb as a maṣdar operator. They differ by hamza seat, mood assigned, and verb count — collapsing them mis-assigns both case and clause structure.
forbidden_transitions.correct_instead: adāt sharṭ jāzima → conditional 'if' tajzimu fiʿlayn
forbidden_transitions.guard: hamza-below light = conditional إِنْ; hamza-above light = subjunctive أَنْ — re-read with norm_strict if the seat is gone

general_rules: Fire a transition ONLY when its context_trigger is observed; an unfired token holds at pending with the stated pending_reason. PENDING beats a wrong role.

Any transition whose two_vote_trigger appears in ../rules/two-vote-required-rules.json two_vote_triggers (irab, case_or_mood, istithna, nafy_lil_jins, idafa_ambiguous) must carry requires_two_vote:true and may not be authored/applied on a single check.

Homograph collisions are resolved upstream by ../rules/particle-context-rules.json (haraka) before a transition fires; forbidden_transitions are the backstop so a swapped sibling role can never be authored even with the diacritic present.
Read names and seats with norm_strict/bare/QAC POS, never the recall-only norm(); a collapsed hamza seat or missing shadda is itself a pending(seat_collapsed | homograph_haraka), not a guess.
